chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI. C.P. No. D-4380 of 2025 ____________________________________________________________ Dated Order with signature of Judge. ____________________________________________________________ Before: Mr. Justice Adnan-ul_Karim Memon Mr. Justice Zulfiqar Ali Sangi Date of hearing and order:-05.03.2026. Mr. Fasih-uz-Zaman, Advocate for the Petitioner. Mr. Abdul Jalil, A.A.G. Mr. Nazir Ahmed Qureshi, Special Education & Literacy Department. Mr. Jawed Ali Khowaja, Focal Person, Law, Govt: of Sindh. Mr. Ghazanfar Ali Abbasi, DS-III, Board of Revenue, Sindh. Mr. Mushtaque Ali, Focal Person for BOR, Sindh. Mr. Nasir Ali Assistant Accountant General --------------- O R D E R Adnan-ul_Karim Memo, J Petitioner Mst. Safia Parveen filed this petition under Article 199 seeking restoration of her stopped salary and correction of her data in the SAP System. 2. It is the case of the Petitioner that she is a permanent Primary School Teacher appointed in 1993 and currently posted at GBPS Rahmania, Karachi, filed this petition under Article 199 seeking restoration of her stopped salary and correction of her data in the SAP System. Her salary has been unlawfully withheld by the Accountant General Sindh since March 2021, despite confirmation from the School Education Department regarding her genuine employment and regular performance of duties. 3. The petitioner’s counsel submitted that all lawful directions issued by competent authorities, including letters dated 19-07-2023 and 22-01-2025, to correct her data and release her salary have been ignored by Respondent No.4, demonstrating malafide, arbitrary action, and abuse of discretion.

Her salary has been unlawfully withheld by the Accountant General Sindh since March 2021, despite confirmation from the School Education Department regarding her genuine employment and regular performance of duties. 3. The petitioner’s counsel submitted that all lawful directions issued by competent authorities, including letters dated 19-07-2023 and 22-01-2025, to correct her data and release her salary have been ignored by Respondent No.4, demonstrating malafide, arbitrary action, and abuse of discretion. The petitioner, being a cancer patient, has suffered serious financial and health-related hardships due to the unlawful stoppage. It was argued that no disciplinary proceedings or lawful justification exist for withholding her salary, and similar employees continue to receive their pay, indicating discriminatory treatment. The petitioner seeks a declaration that the stoppage of her salary is illegal and unconstitutional, and seek directions to correct her data in the SAP System, and immediate release of her salary and arrears, along with any other relief deemed just. 4. The AAG initially submitted that her salary was withheld by the Accountant General’s office due to certain document issues and alleged impersonation. However, the Secretary of Education present in Court assured this Court that the matter would be resolved through a speaking order within one week by hearing the petitioner and all concerned. The request seems to be reasonable and acceded to. In such circumstances, the Education Department and the Accountant General’s office are directed to coordinate to resolve the issue of the petitioner in accordance with the law, and the petition along with pending application(s) stands disposed of accordingly. JUDGE JUDGE Jamil
